updates.summary: Federal Register document (no abstract provided).

updates.title: Safety Zones; Northern California and Lake Tahoe Area Annual Fireworks Events
updates.summary: The Coast Guard will enforce multiple safety zones codified in federal regulations for recurring fireworks events taking place within the San Francisco Captain of the Port area of responsibility. This action is necessary to protect personnel, vessels, and the marine environment from the hazards associated with the fireworks displays. updates.title: Safety Zone; San Pedro Channel, Newport Beach, CA
updates.summary: The Coast Guard is establishing a temporary safety zone for navigable waters in the vicinity of Newport Beach, CA on July 4, 2026. The safety zone is needed to protect personnel, vessels, and the marine environment from potential hazards associated with an overwater fireworks display. Entry of vessels or persons into this zone is prohibited unless specifically authorized by the Captain of the Port, Los Angeles--Long

updates.title: Federal Plan Requirements for Other Solid Waste Incineration Units That Commenced Construction on or Before August 31, 2020, and Have Not Been Modified or Reconstructed After August 29, 2025
updates.summary: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) is proposing a Federal plan to implement the revised emission guidelines for existing other solid waste incineration (OSWI) units. The EPA promulgated emission guidelines for existing OSWI units in 2005 and revised the emission guidelines on June 30, 2025. updates.title: Safety Zone; Isthmus Cove, Santa Catalina Island, CA
updates.summary: The Coast Guard is establishing a temporary safety zone for navigable waters in Isthmus Cove, Santa Catalina Island, CA on July 3, 2026. The safety zone is needed to protect personnel, vessels, and the marine environment from potential hazards associated with an overwater fireworks display. updates.title: Airworthiness Directives; Rolls-Royce Deutschland Ltd & Co KG Engines
updates.summary: The FAA is superseding Airworthiness Directive (AD) 2024-25- 10, which applied to certain Rolls-Royce Deutschland Ltd & Co KG (RRD) Model Trent XWB-97 engines. AD 2024-25-10 required a one-time set of visual and dimensional inspections of the main fuel hose assembly of the fuel manifold to confirm softness, compliance, and lack of resistance, and for shrinkage, cracks, chafing, dents, kinks, necking, and degradation

updates.title: Government Owned Invention Available for License: A Conserved Viral Peptide for Use in Cancer Immunotherapy
updates.summary: The National Cancer Institute (NCI) seeks research co- development partners and/or licensees for viral peptide (CE1)-based therapeutics for HCC prevention and treatment.

updates.title: Government Owned Invention Available for License: Chimeric VLP Vaccines To Prevent HTLV-1 Infection
updates.summary: The National Cancer Institute (NCI) seeks research co- development partners and/or licensees for Chimeric VLP Vaccines to Prevent HTLV-1 Infection.

updates.title: Airworthiness Directives; Gulfstream Aerospace Corporation Airplanes
updates.summary: The FAA is adopting a new airworthiness directive (AD) for certain Gulfstream Aerospace Corporation Model GVIII-G700 and GVIII- G800 airplanes. This AD was prompted by a report indicating that a hollow pin cap on the right-side engine aft thrust strut mount interface was found broken due to a missing sleeve bushing. This AD requires a visual inspection of the upper surface of the engine mount to the forward and aft t

updates.title: Airworthiness Directives; The Boeing Company Airplanes
updates.summary: The FAA is adopting a new airworthiness directive (AD) for certain The Boeing Company Model 787-8, 787-9, and 787-10 airplanes. This AD was prompted by reports of door assist handles pulled loose from their lower attach point in the doorway support bracket during pre-flight checks. This AD requires, for certain airplanes, installing a new retainer above the lower keyway of the support bracket assembly and installing

updates.title: Airworthiness Directives; The Boeing Company Airplanes
updates.summary: The FAA is adopting a new airworthiness directive (AD) for certain The Boeing Company Model 747-8F series airplanes. This AD was prompted by reports of cracking in stringers and splice fittings located at stringer splices at multiple body stations. This AD requires an inspection of each free flange of the stringers at the stringer splice for radius fillers at certain fastener locations, an inspection for cracking of

updates.title: Establishment of Class E Airspace; Jewett, TX
updates.summary: This action establishes Class E airspace at Hub Field, Jewett, TX. The FAA is taking this action to support new instrument procedures.

updates.title: Call for Nominations for the Glen Canyon Dam Adaptive Management Work Group
updates.summary: The U.S. Department of the Interior (Interior) proposes to appoint members to the Glen Canyon Dam Adaptive Management Work Group (AMWG). The Secretary of the Interior (Secretary), acting as administrative lead, is soliciting nominations for qualified persons to serve as members of the AMWG.
